elements.text: Ash drifts through the skeletal remains of an ironwood grove, painting the late evening twilight in shades of bruised grey. Kaelen stands near a jagged outcrop, his eyes scanning the horizon with steady, rhythmic precision.
Renna emerges from the haze, her breath rattling behind her dirt-smudged respirator. She pulls the cloth down, coughing once before dropping a heavy metal scroll-case at his feet.
You wanted whispers, Architect. I brought you a deafening roar.
Quantify the findings, Renna. I require scale, not metaphor.
Renna crouches, kicking the dust away to reveal a crudely scratched map in the ash. Her fingers trace three distinct trade routes, all ending in jagged, burnt-out crosses.
The border camps? Gone. The scrap-merchants? Slaughtered or conscripted. It's not a rival gang expanding territory. It's a tide.
An organized sect. Identify their core leadership and tactical doctrine.

They don't negotiate, Kaelen. They march under a single standard, killing anyone who doesn't kneel within ten heartbeats. My best scout barely made it back to the low-depths before his lungs turned to glass. They are moving fast. Faster than you can plan for.

Kaelen looks down at the scratched map. His analytical mind processes the coordinates of the destruction, realizing the progression isn't random. It is a systematic, brutal siege of the entire region.
The local traditionalists and their petty squabbles are irrelevant now. We are no longer managing a local equilibrium.
We're rats in a collapsing cellar, Kaelen. If that sect reaches the ridge, our little alliance won't even be a speed bump to them.
Kaelen raises his gaze to the dark, soot-choked mountains in the distance, his eyes reflecting the cold, distant stars. The localized chess game has ended; a war of absolute survival has begun.
